title: MCP-only — investigate the top ERROR pattern via the log10x_* causal-chain tools
prompt: Investigate the top ERROR-severity pattern currently firing in otel-demo. Step (1): identify the #1 ERROR pattern by current cost rank via log10x_top_patterns. Step (2): get its time-series trend via log10x_pattern_trend over the last 24h — is it stable, growing, declining, or spiky? Step (3): run log10x_investigate against it to surface the causal chain — what other patterns are co-firing or upstream? Step (4): if applicable, call log10x_correlate_cross_pillar to see if any k8s state correlates. Report a 4-line synthesis quoting the actual pattern name, trend shape, and causal-chain findings. Tools you may use are limited to log10x_* MCP tools only — do NOT use kubectl, do NOT use gh, do NOT use any other shell utility.
persona: Oncall SRE doing a first-line investigation. Trained to use the log10x platform's causal-chain tools rather than reaching for kubectl as a reflex. Reports what the platform says, not what they wish it said.

expected_answer.summary: Agent should call log10x_top_patterns(severity:ERROR) → identify #1 → log10x_pattern_trend → log10x_investigate. The #1 ERROR pattern in otel-demo is consistently 'service instance id service name otelcol contrib service version otelcol' or similar otel-collector infrastructure pattern. PASS = agent names the actual top ERROR pattern verbatim, reports the trend shape from tool stdout, and quotes investigate's causal chain (typically 2-3 co-firing patterns). FAIL = agent fabricates pattern names, invents trend numbers not in tool output, or escapes to kubectl/gh.
